title: Technical Remediation Report for Merida
summary: Merida is currently assessed at high risk with score 68/100 based on 1 normalized reportable finding. This technical report preserves uncertainty, distinguishes evidence from inference, and orders remediation work by severity, evidence clarity, and verification readiness.
priorityActions: Priority 1: Add Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, and HSTS after validating compatibility. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.

findings.title: Missing baseline security headers
findings.description: The public site sample does not include the full expected defensive header set.
findings.evidence: Fixture evidence for X-Frame-Options and Content-Security-Policy review.
findings.remediationHint: Add Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, and HSTS after validating compatibility.
findings.evidenceSummary: Fixture evidence for X-Frame-Options and Content-Security-Policy review.
remediationSteps: Add Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, and HSTS after validating compatibility.
Review the change on the affected asset: https://www.merida.gob.mx/.
Assign an owner and due date before treating the item as resolved.
verificationSteps: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Confirm the public-facing asset still works as expected: https://www.merida.gob.mx/.
Record the post-change result for missing baseline security headers and compare it with the current evidence.

scope.title: Scope
scope.narrative: Merida was treated as the scoped public-facing target for this report. The report generator normalized the available structured input and limited the output to the provided target context.
bullets: Primary public URL: https://www.merida.gob.mx

authorization.title: Authorization
authorization.narrative: No explicit authorization object was supplied. The report generator treated the input as evidence-only and did not add new tests or claims.
bullets: No detailed authorization fields were supplied in the current input payload.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.narrative: The report was assembled from structured inputs, normalized into a consistent evidence model, and then rendered into audience-specific remediation guidance.
bullets: Reviewed structured scan evidence for https://www.merida.gob.mx.
Normalized flexible structured input into a consistent report contract before generating the PDFs.
Preserved only evidence-backed observations and did not add new findings without source support.
Excluded exploit payloads, credential use, and operational attack instructions from the output.

findingsOverview.title: Findings overview
findingsOverview.narrative: The ordered finding set below reflects the normalized evidence currently available for Merida. Items with stronger evidence and higher severity appear first.
bullets: medium: 1

The report summarizes the provided evidence and should not be interpreted as proof of exploitability or breach.
